title: Town Landmark
description: A city-level landmark that stands INSIDE a settlement — a building or district worth a name. Format is 'Name: what it is', templated for variety.
entries: The {pick:Grand|Old|High|Marble|Gilded} {pick:Cathedral|Temple|Basilica|Sanctum}: {pick:its bells are heard for miles|gilded and proud above the rooftops|crumbling but fiercely beloved|the tallest thing for a day's ride|lit day and night by a thousand candles}.

The {pick:Shrine|Chapel|Reliquary} of {pick:the Drowned Saint|the Nine|the Grey Mother|the Last King}: {pick:pilgrims leave tokens at its door|said to weep on holy days|tended by a single ancient priest|older than the town around it}.

The {pick:Guildhall|Merchants' Hall|Hall of Trades}: {pick:where the town's real power sits|banners of a dozen guilds hang from its rafters|its doors are studded with the marks of old bargains|feasts and feuds both begin here}.

The {pick:Town Hall|Moot Hall|Council House}: {pick:its steps are where proclamations are read|a great bell summons the town to it|clerks and petitioners crowd it from dawn|the records of every soul are kept within}.

The {pick:Courthouse|Hall of Justice|Magistrate's Seat}: {pick:the scales above its door are real gold|no one enters it gladly|its cells run deeper than its halls|justice here is swift and rarely kind}.

The {pick:Citadel|Keep|Old Fort|Stronghold}: {pick:it glowers over the lower town|the garrison drills in its yard at dawn|its guns have not fired in living memory|banners snap from its highest tower}.

The {pick:city walls|old ramparts|curtain wall}: {pick:thick enough to drive a cart along the top|patched in a dozen different stones and eras|crumbling on the landward side where no one watches|hung with the standards of the watch}.

The {pick:Great Gate|King's Gate|River Gate|Sun Gate}: {pick:its iron portcullis has rusted half-open|toll-takers work it day and night|carved with a battle no one remembers winning|shut at dusk to the sound of a great horn}.

The {pick:barracks|watch-house|garrison yard}: {pick:the town watch musters here|loud with drills and louder with dice|its armoury is the town's true treasury|off-duty soldiers spill into the taverns around it}.

The {pick:Arena|Fighting Pits|Coliseum}: {pick:blood-sport draws crowds from three counties|its champions are local heroes|closed by law, and busier than ever|the cheapest seats smell of the beasts below}.

The {pick:Grand Market|Great Bazaar|Merchant Square}: {pick:you can buy anything here, and be cheated on all of it|awnings turn the streets to a roofed maze of colour|it never truly closes|the din carries to the edge of town}.

The {pick:Exchange|Counting House|Bourse}: {pick:fortunes are made and unmade before noon|its ledgers move more coin than the treasury|guarded like a fortress, and rightly|the town's heartbeat is the tally of its bells}.
The {pick:docks|wharves|river quay}: {pick:a forest of masts and a babble of tongues|cranes groan over the water day and night|where the town meets the wider world|rough, rich, and never quiet}.

The {pick:caravanserai|carters' yard|great stables}: {pick:a walled yard where the long roads end|beasts and bales and travellers from everywhere|the first place any newcomer is seen|gossip arrives here before it reaches the square}.

The {pick:Great Library|Athenaeum|Archive}: {pick:its stacks are said to hold a book on everything|scholars come from far to read here|dust, candlelight, and the smell of old vellum|its keeper knows more than the council does}.

The {pick:University|College|Academy}: {pick:its students are the town's pride and its plague|robed masters argue in its cloisters|half tavern-brawl, half philosophy|its towers are the tallest after the temple}.

The {pick:Observatory|Astrologers' Tower|Star-House}: {pick:its great brass instruments track the heavens|the town sets its calendar by it|its keeper is consulted before any great venture|lit by a strange lamp on clear nights}.

The {pick:public baths|hot springs|bath-house}: {pick:the whole town is cleaner and slower for it|deals are struck in its steam more than any hall|open to all, from beggar to burgher|its waters are said to cure what ails you}.

The {pick:pleasure gardens|arboretum|green}: {pick:terraces of flowers climb above the rooftops|lovers and duellists both favour it at dusk|the one quiet place in a loud town|its old trees are older than the walls}.
The {pick:playhouse|amphitheatre|opera}: {pick:its troupe is famous three counties over|scandal and spectacle in equal measure|the cheap gallery is the loudest critic|kings have wept in its boxes}.

The {pick:great forge|foundry|smith-row}: {pick:the ring of hammers never fully stops|its chimneys stain the north quarter black|the town's blades are prized far away|heat rolls off it into the streets}.

The {pick:mills|grain-halls|granaries}: {pick:the town's bread and its safety both stored here|great wheels turn day and night on the race|guarded against fire and famine alike|the miller is quietly one of the richest folk in town}.

The {pick:tannery row|dyers' lane|shambles}: {pick:you smell it long before you reach it|the town keeps it downwind and downriver|rich, filthy work that everyone needs and no one praises|its workers keep to their own quarter}.

The {pick:Victory Column|Triumphal Arch|War Memorial}: {pick:it names the dead of a battle still argued over|pigeons and old soldiers gather at its foot|wreaths are laid here every year|the figure atop it has lost its face to weather}.

The {pick:great fountain|public cistern|town well}: {pick:the heart of the square and its gossip|carved with beasts that spout the water|the whole town draws life from it|coins glint in its basin, wishes and all}.

The {pick:clock tower|bell tower|carillon}: {pick:the town runs on its hours, more or less|its chimes are the sound everyone knows in their sleep|its mechanism is a marvel and a mystery|it has been a little slow for a generation}.

The {pick:gallows square|scaffold|pillory}: {pick:the town's grim theatre on hanging days|children play around it when it stands empty|a warning kept deliberately in plain sight|the one place beggars will not sleep}.

The {pick:old prison|gaol|debtors' tower}: {pick:its foundations are older than the town's charter|no one speaks of the deepest cells|escape from it is the stuff of ballads|its keeper is feared more than the magistrate}.

The {pick:thieves' quarter|rookery|warren}: {pick:the watch does not go there after dark|a maze of alleys the town map does not admit exists|ruled by someone whose name is only whispered|everything stolen in town passes through it once}.

The {pick:slums|poor quarter|undercity}: {pick:crammed into the ground the rich would not build on|its people are the town's true engine|a fire or a fever here becomes everyone's problem|proud, dangerous, and overlooked}.

The {pick:plague quarter|lazaret|fever-houses}: {pick:walled off in a bad year and never reopened|the town crosses the street to avoid its lane|its bell tolled too often, once|slowly being reclaimed, house by uneasy house}.

The {pick:ruined district|burned quarter|old town}: {pick:a fire, a flood, or a war left it hollow|children dare each other into its shells|the town has simply grown around the scar|something has started living there again}.

The {pick:menagerie|beast-garden|aviary}: {pick:creatures from lands the townsfolk will never see|a nobleman's vanity, open to the public on feast days|its keeper speaks to the animals and they seem to answer|the roaring at night unsettles the whole quarter}.

The {pick:harbour lighthouse|beacon tower|pharos}: {pick:its fire is never allowed to die|it has guided ships home for centuries|the keeper's family has held the post for generations|a landmark seen long before the town itself}.

The {pick:aqueduct|water-stair|conduit}: {pick:it carries the mountains' meltwater into every fountain|its arches stride across the valley to the town|an old marvel the town no longer knows how to repair|children walk its channel on a dare}.

The {pick:necropolis|catacombs|tombs}: {pick:the town's dead outnumber its living, below|its gates open only for funerals|a city beneath the city, and older|its caretakers know every name carved there}.
